zh.lifeSummary: 當James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran於1519年1月1日出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾漢密爾頓時，他的父親Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan43歲，母親Janet Bethune33歲。他於1532年9月23日與Lady Margaret Douglas在蘇格蘭布特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他在蘇格蘭被列為governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots。他去世於1575年1月22日在他的家鄉享年56歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭斯林史爾法爾吉可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran于1519年1月1日出生在苏格兰藍納可爾漢密爾頓时，他的父亲Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan43岁，母亲Janet Bethune33岁。他于1532年9月23日与Lady Margaret Douglas在苏格兰布特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他在苏格兰被列为governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots。他去世于1575年1月22日在他的家乡享年56岁，埋葬在苏格兰斯林史爾法爾吉可。

nameMeanings.name: Hamilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from what is now a deserted village in the parish of Barkby, Leicestershire. This is named from Old English hamel ‘crooked’ + dūn ‘hill’. Hamilton near Glasgow was founded by the English Hamiltons and named after them, and later bearers took their surname from the Scottish place. In the north of Ireland, where many Hamiltons settled in the 17th century, this surname may sometimes have been adopted for Hamill . It has also been adopted as an Anglicized form of southern Irish Ó hUrmholtaigh.

History: This name is borne by one of the most distinguished families of the Scottish nobility; they hold many titles, including marquessate and dukedom of Hamilton, the marquessate of Douglas, the dukedom of Abercorn, and the earldom of Haddington. They are descended from Walter FitzGilbert de Hameldone, a Norman baron who gave his support to Robert the Bruce in the 13th century. A member of this family was Sir William Hamilton (1730–1803), a British diplomat and archaeologist, whose wife, Lady Emma Hamilton (c. 1765–1815), became the mistress of Admiral Horatio Nelson. A branch of the family was established in Ireland by Sir Frederick Hamilton (died 1646), who served in the Swedish army of Gustavus Adolphus. He later became governor of Ulster, and his descendants were created viscounts Boyne. The family have given their name to Newtownhamilton and Hamiltonsbaron in County Armagh. Another branch of the family were to be found in Denmark, where Henrik Albertsen Hamilton (1588–1648) was a noted Latin poet. A 17th-century example of a Hamilton from Glasgow, Scotland, is recorded in the Netherlands, where the name is found in the form Hamelton. Another Scottish Hamilton, James, went to the West Indies in the 18th century and was the father of Alexander Hamilton (1755–1804), who arrived in NY in 1772 and became the first US secretary of the Treasury.

variants.name: Hamill
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Haineville or Henneville in Manche, France, named from the ancient Germanic personal name Hagano + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
English (Lancashire): nickname for a maimed person or someone with a distinctive scar, from Middle English, Old English hamel ‘mutilated, scarred’.
Irish (Ulster): according to MacLysaght, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÁdhmaill ‘descendant of Ádhmall’, which he derives from ádhmall ‘active’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran was born on 1 January 1519, in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan, was 43 and his mother, Janet Bethune, was 33. He married Lady Margaret Douglas on 23 September 1532, in Bute, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. His occupation is listed as governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots in Scotland. He died on 22 January 1575, in his hometown, at the age of 56, and was buried in Falkirk, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran est né le 1 janvier 1519, à Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Janet Bethune, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret Douglas le 23 septembre 1532, à Bute, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots à Écosse. Il est décédé le 22 janvier 1575, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Falkirk, Stirlingshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran am 1. Januar 1519 in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan 43 und seine Mutter Janet Bethune 33. Er hat am 23. September 1532 in Bute, Scotland Lady Margaret Douglas geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots in Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 22. Januar 1575 im Alter von 56 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Falkirk, Stirlingshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran è nato il il 1 gennaio 1519 nel Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Janet Bethune, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Lady Margaret Douglas il 23 settembre 1532, nel Bute, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots nel Scotland. Morì il 22 gennaio 1575, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Falkirk, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arranが1519年1月1日にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアハミルトンで生まれた時，父Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanは43歳，母Janet Bethuneは33歳であった。1532年9月23日にスコットランドブトでLady Margaret Douglasと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。スコットランドでの職業はgovernor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scotsと記録されている。彼は1575年1月22日に故郷で56歳で死去し，スコットランドスティルリングシュアイアファルキルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran(이)가 1519년 1월 1일 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 함이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet Bethune(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1532년 9월 23일 스컷랜드 부트에서 Lady Margaret Douglas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 스컷랜드에서 governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots이었다. 그는 1575년 1월 22일 그의 고향에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 파커크에 묻혔다.

pt.1 January 1519–22 January 1575: 1 de janeiro de 1519–22 de janeiro de 1575
pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1519, em Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Janet Bethune, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Margaret Douglas em 23 de setembro de 1532, em Bute, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots em Scotland. Ele faleceu em 22 de janeiro de 1575, em sua cidade natal, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Falkirk, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran родился 1 января 1519 г. в местности хамилтон, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Janet Bethune, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret Douglas 23 сентября 1532 г. в местности бут, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots в местности Шотландия. Он умер 22 января 1575 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности фалкирк, стирлингшир, Шотландия.

sm.from 1537 to 1538: mai le 1537 e o'o I le 1538

es.1 January 1519–22 January 1575: 1 de enero de 1519–22 de enero de 1575
es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Hamilton Duke of Chatellerault, Second Earl of Arran nació el 1 de enero de 1519, en Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lan, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Janet Bethune, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Lady Margaret Douglas el 23 de septiembre de 1532, en Bute, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era governor of scotland- during the minority of mary, queen of scots en Scotland. Murió el 22 de enero de 1575, en su pueblo, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Falkirk, Stirlingshire, Scotland.
